eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a $70-80 million domestic opening weekend for The Devil Wears Prada 2 at 95.2% implied probability, propelled by Thursday previews exceeding $10 million and a stellar $32.5-33 million Friday haul that aligns precisely with pre-release tracking estimates of $75-80 million. The sequel's star power—Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci reprising their roles—fuels nostalgia-driven presales and buzz, bolstered by certified fresh 76-78% Rotten Tomatoes critics' score and an A- CinemaScore surpassing the original's B. Positive word-of-mouth could propel an upset into the 80-90 million range with strong Saturday-Sunday multipliers, while softer holds amid competition from holdover Michael might trim it under $70 million, though audience enthusiasm makes that improbable as final weekend figures finalize Monday.

question: Will Ségolène Royal win the 2027 French presidential election?
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Jordan Bardella slightly ahead of Édouard Philippe in first-round voting intentions for France's 2027 presidential election, reflecting late March 2026 polls where the National Rally leader topped scenarios at 34-38% amid widespread desire for political change following inconclusive municipal elections. Philippe, bolstered by his Le Havre mayoral reelection, trails closely at 18-25% as the center-right Horizons figure best positioned to defeat Bardella in runoff simulations (51.5-48.5%). The race remains tightly contested due to a fragmented field—divided left with Jean-Luc Mélenchon at 11-15%, multiple center-right rivals like Bruno Retailleau, and no party primaries yet—splitting anti-RN votes. Separation could arise from candidate designations by autumn 2026, potential center consolidation, or economic shocks before the April 2027 first round.

question: Sharjah announces secession from UAE by May 8?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Ruler of Sharjah, the Sharjah Executive Council, or another official Sharjah governing authority officially and definitively announces that the Emirate of Sharjah, or a majority of the territory of Sharjah, will leave, secede from, or otherwise cease to be part of the United Arab Emirates by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying announcement may state that Sharjah will become an independent state, join another state, or otherwise cease to be a constituent emirate of the United Arab Emirates.

Only official and definitive announcements will qualify. Suggestions, statements of consideration, calls for independence, rumors, unofficial reports, social media claims, or other non-definitive statements will not qualify.

Routine border adjustments, municipal reorganizations, or changes to Sharjah’s local governance will not qualify unless they explicitly declare that Sharjah, or a majority of the territory of Sharjah, will leave or cease to be part of the United Arab Emirates.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Ruler of Sharjah, the Sharjah Executive Council, or other official Sharjah government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects extreme skepticism toward rumors of Sharjah seceding from the UAE, driven by debunked social media hoaxes originating around mid-April 2026 amid heightened Gulf tensions from the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict. No official statements from Sharjah Ruler Sheikh Dr. Sultan bin Muhammad Al Qasimi or UAE federal authorities support independence claims, which featured fabricated maps and exaggerated territorial assertions. The UAE's stable federal structure, enshrined in its 1971 constitution uniting seven emirates, imposes significant legal and political barriers to unilateral secession without consensus among rulers. Absent verifiable diplomatic rifts or institutional breakdowns, markets await any primary announcements, with no scheduled events on the horizon to alter this status quo.

question: Will the Chicago White Sox win the 2026 World Series?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB World Series. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 MLB World Series per the rules of MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from MLB (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Los Angeles Dodgers command trader consensus at 28.5% implied probability to repeat as World Series champions, bolstered by their unmatched roster depth, defending champion pedigree, and early-season dominance atop the NL West with a top power ranking after a 19-9 start through late April. New York Yankees follow at 10.5%, riding a league-best 22-11 AL East lead and consistent rise in rankings, while Atlanta Braves hold 10.2% on strong NL contention despite minor injury concerns like Spencer Strider's oblique strain. Seattle Mariners (5.9%) and surging Chicago Cubs (4.2%) reflect pitching strength and momentum in power rankings, differentiating them in a wide-open field vulnerable to midseason trades, injuries like Milwaukee's Brandon Woodruff IL stint, and regression risks.
